query_normalized: investigating the impact of code ownership on llm code understanding computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on instruction-following capabilities and data generation techniques (Evol-Instruct) for LLMs, which does not measure code ownership, code understanding, or the specific mechanism linking ownership to model performance. It fails to satisfy any inclusion criteria (a-f) as it addresses a different research problem (instruction tuning) rather than the user's specific domain of code ownership impact.
